medical_history.chief_complaint: Amniotic band at the fetus’s ankle and toes involving a left thick edematous foot and a right edematous club foot as well as an umbilical cord without abnormal blood flow
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented at 18 weeks’ gestation with amniotic band at the fetus’s ankle and toes. All toes were visible on both feet. Distal arterial and venous blood flow was present within the edematous feet. Movement of the ankles, including flexion, could not be visualized during an extensive ultrasound examination. The position of the feet in utero remained unchanged during 3 consecutive ultrasound examinations over 2 weeks.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Left foot appeared pale and edematous in its distal portion. The right foot presented with 2 necrotic toes. Multiple amniotic sheets were observed, twisted and wrapped around the left ankle, the umbilical cord, and the right clubfoot.
